metadata.name: Windbrisk Heights Hideaway ETB trigger: exile top card face-down
metadata.description: Tests the full Hideaway (CR 702.75a) ETB lifecycle for Windbrisk Heights. p1 plays Windbrisk Heights; it enters tapped (CR 614.1c / 702.75b ETB replacement). The Hideaway 4 trigger fires: HideawayTrigger is placed on the stack. All players pass priority. HideawayTrigger resolves: the engine looks at the top 4 cards of p1's library (Lightning Bolt, Grizzly Bears, Llanowar Elves, Sol Ring), exiles Lightning Bolt face-down with exiled_by_hideaway set to the Windbrisk Heights ObjectId, and puts the other 3 on the bottom of the library. Final state: Windbrisk Heights on p1's battlefield (tapped), 1 card in exile, library has 2 cards remaining (Forest and Mountain were below the top 4), stack empty.
metadata.generation_notes: CR 702.75a: When Windbrisk Heights enters, the Hideaway 4 trigger fires. The engine's check_triggers detects KeywordAbility::Hideaway(4) on the entering permanent and queues a HideawayTrigger (is_hideaway_trigger=true, hideaway_count=4) on PendingTrigger. flush_pending_triggers places HideawayTrigger on the stack. When it resolves, resolution.rs:HideawayTrigger arm looks at the top hideaway_count cards of the controller's library, exiles the first one face-down (face_down=true, exiled_by_hideaway=source ObjectId), and calls move_object_to_bottom_of_zone for the remaining cards. CR 702.75b: older Hideaway cards are errata'd to 'Hideaway N' plus a separate 'enters tapped' line modeled as AbilityDefinition::Replacement{EntersTapped, is_self:true}. Engine fix applied: Command::PlayLand now calls check_triggers + flush_pending_triggers (CR 603.3), matching the pattern used by CastSpell and ActivateAbility. Previously, ETB triggers on lands were silently dropped.

script.step_note: p1's first main phase. p1 plays Windbrisk Heights as their land play for the turn.

actions.description: Initial: Windbrisk Heights in p1 hand, library has 6 cards, exile empty

actions.note: CR 702.75b: p1 plays Windbrisk Heights. The EntersTapped replacement fires: the land enters tapped (CR 614.1c). After the land enters, check_triggers detects KeywordAbility::Hideaway(4) on the ETB permanent and queues a HideawayTrigger. flush_pending_triggers places it on the stack. CR 603.3: triggered abilities are placed on the stack after the action that caused them.

actions.description: After play_land: Windbrisk Heights on p1 battlefield (tapped), hand empty, HideawayTrigger on stack
actions.note: Windbrisk Heights enters tapped. The Hideaway 4 ETB trigger is now on the stack waiting to resolve.
All players pass priority. With all players having passed and the stack non-empty, the top item (HideawayTrigger) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 702.75a: HideawayTrigger resolves. The engine looks at the top 4 cards (Lightning Bolt, Grizzly Bears, Llanowar Elves, Sol Ring). The first card (Lightning Bolt) is exiled face-down with face_down=true and exiled_by_hideaway=Windbrisk Heights ObjectId (CR 607.2a linked ability). The other 3 cards are put on the bottom of the library in a random order.

The remaining 3 cards (Grizzly Bears, Llanowar Elves, Sol Ring) are put on the bottom of p1's library in a random order. Library now has 5 cards: Forest, Mountain (original bottom) + the 3 returned cards.

actions.note: HideawayTrigger resolves. 1 card exiled face-down, 3 cards go to the bottom of the library.

actions.description: Final: Windbrisk Heights on battlefield (tapped), 1 card in exile, library has 5 cards, stack empty
actions.note: CR 702.75a: One card is exiled face-down (Lightning Bolt). The 3 remaining top cards are on the bottom. Library: was 6 (Lightning Bolt + Grizzly Bears + Llanowar Elves + Sol Ring + Forest + Mountain) → exiled 1 → 5 remain. Windbrisk Heights is on the battlefield tapped. The exiled card's exiled_by_hideaway field links it to Windbrisk Heights for the play ability (CR 607.2a).
